title: Cienfuegosia drummondii
intro: Cienfuegosia drummondii, also known as Drummond's passionflower or Passionflower vine, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and the southern United States, characterized by its distinctive tendrils and intricate flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, and its health benefits are attributed to its sedative and anti-anxiety effects. The bioactive constituents of Cienfuegosia drummondii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, with the most common preparations being passionflower tea and passionflower extract.
taxonomy: Cienfuegosia drummondii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Cienfuegosia, which is a group of plants native to the Americas. This plant is further classified as a member of the order Malvales, which comprises various flowering plants with similar characteristics. The classification of Cienfuegosia drummondii is based on its physical and genetic features, which distinguish it from other related species. The Malvaceae family, to which Cienfuegosia drummondii belongs, is a large and diverse group of plants that includes hibiscus, hollyhock, and okra.
